---
title: "The La Selva-Barva MetaNetwork"
format: 
  html:
    css: styles.css
    page-layout: full
---

::: {style="text-align:center"}
Understanding tropical forest communities through species interactions
:::

::: {.columns .v-center}

::: {.columns style="width:100%;"}

::: {.column width="40%" .fade-in}

## The La Selva-Volcán Barva Transect

As one of the best-preserved and most-studied elevational gradients in the tropics, this 2,900m elevational gradient provides **one of the best sources of ecological data in the tropics.**

:::

::: {.column width="60%" .fade-in}

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5

library(sf)
library(ggplot2)

VB <- st_read(
  here::here("../LaSelva-MetaNetwork-data/data/L0/shapefiles/VB.shp"),
  quiet = TRUE
)

la_selva <- st_read(
  here::here("../LaSelva-MetaNetwork-data/data/L0/shapefiles/la_selva.shp"),
  quiet = TRUE
)

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = VB, aes(fill = ID)) +
  geom_sf(data = la_selva, aes(fill = NAME)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("gray80", "gray90"),
    labels = c(
      "La Selva Biological Station",
      "Braulio Carrillo National Park"
    )
  ) +
  labs(fill = "") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.1, "cm")
  )
```
:::

::: {style="text-align:center"}
## The MetaNetwork
:::
